title: Control CUI Posted or Processed on Publicly Accessible Systems
description: control CUI posted or processed on publicly accessible systems

checks.name: Individuals Authorized to Post to Publicly Accessible Systems are Identified
checks.purpose: Identification of individuals authorized to post to publicly accessible systems is an organizational GRC documentation concern documented in acceptable use and content management policies and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Client

checks.name: Procedures to Ensure CUI is Not Posted to Publicly Accessible Systems are Identified
checks.purpose: Identification of procedures ensuring CUI is not posted to publicly accessible systems is an organizational GRC documentation concern documented in content management policies and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Client

checks.name: A Review Process is in Place Prior to Posting Content to Publicly Accessible Systems
checks.purpose: Establishment of a content review process prior to posting is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Client

checks.name: Content on Publicly Accessible Systems is Reviewed to Ensure it Does Not Include CUI
checks.purpose: Review of publicly accessible content for CUI is an organizational process concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Client

checks.name: Mechanisms are in Place to Remove and Address Improper Posting of CUI
checks.purpose: Verify that technical mechanisms exist to detect and address improper CUI posting including audit logging of file system changes, DLP policy enforcement, and removable media write restrictions that limit unauthorized content transfer to public systems

checks.name: Individuals Authorized to Post to Publicly Accessible Systems are Identified
checks.purpose: Identification of individuals authorized to post to publicly accessible systems is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Server

checks.name: Procedures to Ensure CUI is Not Posted to Publicly Accessible Systems are Identified
checks.purpose: Identification of procedures ensuring CUI is not posted to publicly accessible systems is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Server

checks.name: A Review Process is in Place Prior to Posting Content to Publicly Accessible Systems
checks.purpose: Establishment of a content review process prior to posting is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Server

checks.name: Content on Publicly Accessible Systems is Reviewed to Ensure it Does Not Include CUI
checks.purpose: Review of publicly accessible content for CUI is an organizational process concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Server

checks.name: Mechanisms are in Place to Remove and Address Improper Posting of CUI
checks.purpose: Verify that technical mechanisms exist on the server to detect and address improper CUI posting including file integrity monitoring of web content directories, audit logging of content changes, and access controls restricting who can modify publicly accessible content

checks.name: Individuals Authorized to Post to Publicly Accessible Systems are Identified
checks.purpose: Identification of individuals authorized to post to publicly accessible systems is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Linux

checks.name: Procedures to Ensure CUI is Not Posted to Publicly Accessible Systems are Identified
checks.purpose: Identification of procedures ensuring CUI is not posted to publicly accessible systems is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Linux

checks.name: A Review Process is in Place Prior to Posting Content to Publicly Accessible Systems
checks.purpose: Establishment of a content review process prior to posting is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Linux

checks.name: Content on Publicly Accessible Systems is Reviewed to Ensure it Does Not Include CUI
checks.purpose: Review of publicly accessible content for CUI is an organizational process concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Linux

checks.name: Mechanisms are in Place to Remove and Address Improper Posting of CUI
checks.purpose: Verify that technical mechanisms exist to detect and address improper CUI posting including auditd monitoring of web content directories, file permission controls restricting write access to public content paths, and SELinux or AppArmor policy confining web server processes

checks.name: Individuals Authorized to Post to Publicly Accessible Systems are Identified
checks.purpose: Identification of individuals authorized to post to publicly accessible systems is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Debian

checks.name: Procedures to Ensure CUI is Not Posted to Publicly Accessible Systems are Identified
checks.purpose: Identification of procedures ensuring CUI is not posted to publicly accessible systems is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Debian

checks.name: A Review Process is in Place Prior to Posting Content to Publicly Accessible Systems
checks.purpose: Establishment of a content review process prior to posting is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Debian

checks.name: Content on Publicly Accessible Systems is Reviewed to Ensure it Does Not Include CUI
checks.purpose: Review of publicly accessible content for CUI is an organizational process concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Debian

checks.name: Mechanisms are in Place to Remove and Address Improper Posting of CUI
checks.purpose: Verify that technical mechanisms exist to detect and address improper CUI posting including auditd monitoring of web content directories, file permission controls restricting write access to public content paths, and AppArmor policy confining web server processes

remediation: Establish and document a content review and approval process that must be completed before any information is posted to publicly accessible systems. Identify and train all individuals authorized to post content on public-facing systems. Implement file integrity monitoring on web content directories using a tool such as auditd on Linux or Windows file auditing to detect unauthorized changes. Restrict write access to public content directories to only authorized publishing accounts via NTFS permissions on Windows or file permissions and ACLs on Linux. Deploy DLP controls to scan content for CUI markers before publication. Establish a removal process so that any CUI identified on a public system can be taken down promptly.
